scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope aerospace engineering-change handoff package with explicit ownership, effectivity, required artifacts, and blocker state.
Missing baseline, effectivity, hold, supplier-control, and destination-acceptance prerequisites were treated as blockers rather than a routable handoff.
Duplicate ownership and conflicting CCB, production, supplier, and logistics timing were escalated with an owner map instead of dropped into generic coordination.
Conflicting PLM, ERP, MES, WMS, and supplier records were preserved as traceability and configuration blockers and not collapsed into a confident answer.
NCR, MRB, and quality-hold posture were preserved and release, deviation, and use-as-is authority stayed with quality and engineering owners.
Supplier process change and substitute material approval were rejected and routed to procurement, supplier-quality, engineering, and customer-flowdown owners.
The lane refused to absorb drawing, inventory, work-order, supplier notification, and CCB closure execution work.
Missing tenant CCB, MRB, customer, export-control, retrieval, and release authority context correctly triggered low-confidence escalation.
Conflicting public baseline, customer flowdown, and tenant release policy were escalated for human resolution instead of synthesized into a definitive instruction.
Aerospace and defense change terminology was normalized into authoritative artifact and status states before routing.
Closure was only confirmed after destination-owner proof and required ECO, effectivity, NCR, FAI, supplier, and implementation artifacts were explicit.
Supplier nonconformance with possible flight-hardware impact was escalated with throughput and traceability context preserved and without unsupported continuation.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, coordination-handoffs-and-routing, and manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 aerospace-and-defense engineering change coordination scenarios.

Runtime remains specification-based and no retained implementation, final engineering approval, quality release, supplier approval, MRB disposition, CCB decision, airworthiness signoff, export-control determination, or direct execution authority is claimed.
